key_evidence: Microsoft Israel GM Alon Haimovich fired (mid-May 2026) following internal investigation into Unit 8200 Azure misuse
Investigation confirmed ~200M hours of Palestinian phone surveillance recordings stored on Azure European servers, used to plan lethal airstrikes and West Bank arrests
Microsoft terminated Unit 8200's Azure access; multiple senior governance managers also departed
Investigation revealed Microsoft Israel subsidiary was not fully transparent with global HQ — structural governance gap
Business & Human Rights Resource Centre confirmed Microsoft's access-termination action as substantive
methodology_novel: Harm-enabled-then-accountability-applied dual-signal weighting: enabling of severe harm (mass surveillance, lethal use over years) partially but not fully offset by substantive accountability response (executive firing, services termination, transparency about governance gap). Net direction is downward but magnitude is sub-threshold because accountability response is the strongest single-company accountability action in the benchmark's recent history. See assessment for precedent details.
